title: Security Groups
overview: Security groups (SGs) allow administrators to assign permissions and access control to collections of users or AD objects like computers. This approach simplifies management by grouping users with similar access needs rather than assigning rights individually.

study.overview: Security groups (SGs) allow administrators to assign permissions and access control to collections of users or AD objects like computers. This approach simplifies management by grouping users with similar access needs rather than assigning rights individually.
study.whySoc: Use this note as an analyst reference: understand the concept, identify the relevant telemetry, pivot through evidence, and document the decision path clearly.
workflow: Identify the user, host, process, network flow, or identity object involved.
Collect the highest-value logs first and preserve timestamps and assumptions.
Pivot through related notes, telemetry sources, and MITRE references.
Document findings, gaps, containment options, and detection improvements.
tips: Note: Security Groups are used for access control, while Organizational Units (OUs) are used for administration and policy application (e.g., applying GPOs).
